title: Premium Processing — Immigration News & Alerts — ELLIS PORTER
content:
As part of the year-end appropriations process, Congress passed legislation yesterday that will permanently expand the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) premium processing program, while also increasing program fees, and the legislation was signed into law by the president today.
After temporarily suspending its premium processing service on March 20, 2020, USCIS announced today that it will gradually reinstate premium processing service for certain I-129 and I-140 filings.
Cap-subject H-1B cases cannot be fast-tracked until further notice.
Procedures for submitting FY2019 H-1B cap-subject cases are not expected to change, according to Service Center officials.
Premium Processing is now back online for all application types.
An official announcement regarding the Premium Processing program is expected at any time.
Those with H-1B visa petitions subject to the FY2018 cap may be breathing a little easier tonight.
Premium processing immediately resumes for certain cap-exempt H-1B petitioners.
Premium processing to resume June 26th for eligible J-1 waiver doctors.
Ombudsman call hints at plans to resume Premium Processing services for H-1Bs.
Some of the latest H-1B related news.
The unexpected suspension of H-1B Premium Processing has left many unanswered questions for clients relying on speedy adjudications to plan for employment and travel in the upcoming months.  Our team of experts at Ellis Porter is here for you to examine these and other questions while planning your personalized strategy.
Yesterday, USCIS announced that it will suspend premium processing for all H-1B applications starting April 3, 2017 and it stated the suspension could last up to six months.
